title: Why niche programming languages are making a comeback
post:
Something unexpected is happening: specialized languages are coming back.

For a decade, the trend was toward general-purpose languages. JavaScript, Python, Go — one language to rule everything. The thinking was: fewer languages means less cognitive overhead.

But AI changes that calculus. When AI handles the boilerplate, the cognitive overhead of context-switching drops dramatically. You can use Elixir for distributed systems, Rust for memory safety, R for statistics — and AI helps you bridge the gaps.

We're entering an era where the right tool for the job actually means something again.

The developers thriving are the ones who know multiple paradigms, not just one language deeply. AI amplifies your ability to be a polyglot.

This is good news for the industry. Specialization drives innovation.

Are you learning any niche languages? Thoughts?
